description_long: This guard rail consists of two sturdy vertical posts connected by a horizontal rail with a decorative crisscross pattern, forming an X-shaped lattice between the posts. The posts are square in cross-section and mounted on broad, flat base plates for stability, suggesting a surface-mounted installation. The rail and lattice are constructed from metal, likely steel, and the entire structure is coated in a uniform dark gray finish. The ends of the posts are capped with flat, square plates, giving a clean and finished appearance. The design is both functional and ornamental, suitable for urban sidewalks, parks, or pedestrian zones to prevent passage while maintaining visibility. There are no visible reflective markings or additional safety features on the rail.
description: A dark gray metal guard rail with square posts and a decorative X-pattern lattice, mounted on flat base plates. The design is both functional and ornamental, suitable for urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two square posts and the narrow profile of the rail, with the lattice pattern not visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full X-pattern lattice between the posts and the flat base plates anchoring the posts.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the posts' alignment and the thinness of the rail structure, with the lattice pattern edge-on.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the X-pattern lattice and the square post caps, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
